name: cf-tdd

criteria.name: red_before_green
criteria.description: A failing test is written and demonstrated before any production code

scoring.0: Production code written first with no failing test phase
scoring.1: Test written first but never shown to fail (jumped straight to passing)
scoring.2: Failing test shown but production code was partially written beforehand
scoring.3: Clear RED phase: test written, run, shown failing, then production code written to pass it

criteria.name: test_quality
criteria.description: Tests are meaningful, testing behavior rather than implementation details

scoring.0: Tests are trivial (e.g., testing that true is true) or test implementation details
scoring.1: Tests cover happy path only with no edge cases or error scenarios
scoring.2: Tests cover main behavior and some edge cases but miss error handling
scoring.3: Tests cover behavior comprehensively: happy path, edge cases, and error scenarios

criteria.name: minimal_code
criteria.description: Production code is the minimum needed to pass the tests (no speculative code)

scoring.0: Large amounts of unused or speculative code beyond what tests require
scoring.1: Some extra code that is not exercised by any test
scoring.2: Code is mostly minimal but includes minor anticipatory abstractions
scoring.3: Every line of production code is driven by a test — no unused code paths

criteria.name: refactor_step
criteria.description: After GREEN, code is refactored while keeping tests passing

scoring.0: No refactoring step — code left in first-pass state
scoring.1: Minor cleanup attempted but tests not re-run after refactoring
scoring.2: Meaningful refactoring done and tests pass, but some duplication remains
scoring.3: Clean refactoring improves structure/readability, all tests verified passing afterward

notes: The RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle should be visible in the output. Multiple cycles for complex features are expected. Score each cycle if multiple are present and average.
